content: arXiv 2603.00151v1 Announce Type  new Abstract  For robots to operate effectively and safely alongside humans, they must be able to understand the progress of ongoing actions. This ability, known as action progress prediction, is critical for tasks ranging from timely assistance to autonomous decision-making. However, modeling action progression in robotics has often been overlooked. Moreover, a single camera may be insufficient for understanding robot's ego-actions, as self-occlusion can significantly hinder perception and model performance. In this paper, we propose a multi-view architecture for action progress prediction in robot manipulation tasks. Experiments on Mobile ALOHA demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

metadata.author: Elena Zoppellari, Federico Becattini, Marco Fiorucci, Lamberto Ballan
metadata.summary: 00151v1 Announce Type  new Abstract  For robots to operate effectively and safely alongside humans, they must be able to understand the progress of ongoing actions
